stories.title: Google Vids now lets you star in your own AI videos
stories.simple_summary: Google is adding a feature where you can create videos with a digital copy of yourself as the main character. You describe what you want the video to do, and the AI creates it.
stories.why_it_matters: This could be useful for small business owners who want to make promotional videos without hiring an actor or video crew—though you'd still need to learn how to use the tool.

stories.title: Roblox launches an AI-powered game creation feature in its mobile app
stories.simple_summary: Roblox (a platform where people make games) is letting creators build simple games by just typing what they want, instead of writing code.
stories.why_it_matters: This matters mainly to game developers and people who enjoy Roblox—not really relevant to most small business owners.

stories.title: New York governor says she’s using AI to analyze ‘every single rule’ in the state
stories.simple_summary: New York's governor is using AI to read through all the state's old rules and regulations to find ones that are outdated or don't make sense anymore.
stories.why_it_matters: This could eventually lead to simpler, updated rules for everyone—but these changes take time and you won't see immediate effects on your business.

stories.title: Europe tells Google it must open Android phones to rivals’ AI bots
stories.simple_summary: European regulators are telling Google it must allow other AI assistants to work on Android phones, instead of only Google's own AI.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly affects tech companies and investors—nothing you need to act on as a small business owner.

stories.title: Atlanta company works with businesses to lower data risks from AI misuse
stories.simple_summary: A company in Atlanta is helping other businesses protect themselves from problems that happen when AI is misused or makes mistakes.
stories.why_it_matters: If your business uses AI tools or stores sensitive customer data, this type of service could help protect you—but it depends on your specific needs.

stories.title: Taxpayer group warns New York's AI data center moratorium could cost jobs and investment
stories.simple_summary: A taxpayer group is warning that New York's decision to stop building new AI data centers could hurt job creation and business investment in the state.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly about politics and policy debate—not something that directly affects your daily business operations.

stories.title: Google’s AI Mode now lets you link and interact with select apps
stories.simple_summary: Google's AI is getting an upgrade so it can do tasks for you across different apps you already use (like calendar, email, or shopping apps).
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Google's tools for business, this could save you time by automating repetitive tasks—though you'd need to set it up and trust it with your information.

stories.title: Google is renaming NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook
stories.simple_summary: Google is renaming one of its AI note-taking apps from NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook, but it still works the same way.
stories.why_it_matters: This is just a name change—nothing you need to do unless you currently use this app.

stories.title: Musk’s xAI sues user who allegedly used Grok to create child sexual abuse material
stories.simple_summary: A company called xAI is suing someone who allegedly misused their AI chatbot called Grok to create illegal child abuse material.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a legal and criminal matter that doesn't directly affect your business—it shows companies are taking action against serious misuse of their AI tools.

stories.title: Yes, you can now order DoorDash from the command line
stories.simple_summary: DoorDash is testing a tool that lets computer code and AI programs order food delivery without a person clicking buttons—just through typing commands.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to software developers and is of interest mainly to investors—not relevant to small business owners unless you run a restaurant using DoorDash.

stories.title: Why is OpenAI selling a ChatGPT basketball?
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI released a basketball with ChatGPT branding as merchandise or a novelty item.
stories.why_it_matters: This is just a fun product a company made—it has no business relevance.

stories.title: Twenty-nine countries sign agreement to establish global AI cooperation body
stories.simple_summary: Twenty-nine countries agreed to work together and create a group that will help set guidelines and rules for how AI should be used globally.
stories.why_it_matters: This is early-stage international cooperation that could eventually affect AI regulations worldwide, but it won't have immediate effects on your business.
